front: Poem: the mariner kills an albatross; the crew hangs it around his neck; Death and Life-in-Death play dice for the crew's souls
back: "The Rime of the Ancient Mariner" (Samuel Taylor Coleridge)
work: The Rime of the Ancient Mariner

front: Poem: the mariner blesses water snakes and the curse begins to lift; the wedding guest 'cannot choose but hear'
back: "The Rime of the Ancient Mariner" (Samuel Taylor Coleridge)
work: The Rime of the Ancient Mariner

front: Poem: 'In Xanadu did Kubla Khan / A stately pleasure-dome decree'; describes 'caverns measureless to man'
Poem: interrupted by a 'person from Porlock' during an opium dream; describes 'A sunny pleasure-dome with caves of ice'
Work: proposes the 'willing suspension of disbelief' and 'esemplastic power' of imagination; distinguishes Primary and Secondary Imagination from Fancy
Work: first chapter prints parodic sonnets by the alter ego Nehemiah Higgenbottom; proposes 'esemplastic power' to counter David Hartley
Poem: opens with the title phenomenon's 'secret ministry'; declares 'all seasons shall be sweet' to the poet's infant son
Author: planned the utopian community 'Pantisocracy' with brother-in-law Robert Southey
